---
title: "TODO"
output: html_document
editor_options: 
  markdown: 
    wrap: 72
---

## functions to add

**v0.1.0-beta** **Public release**

1.  Alex to dos

    1.  ~~fix trait-reading code in `runOccJSDM()`: it checks
        `data$traits`, not `data$traitsMatrix` or the unused
        `traitsMatrix` function argument. This currently works only
        because R's `$` partially matches list names (`traitsMatrix` is
        the only element of `data` starting with `"traits"`), which is
        fragile.~~ **fixed**: the source now references `data$traits`
        explicitly (no more reliance on partial matching). The
        `traitsMatrix` function argument is still unused/unwired
        (defaults to `NULL`) -- minor leftover, not the fragility bug
        itself.

    2.  ~~gitignore and stop tracking `src/occPlus.dll` (also filename
        should be changed on Alex's end)~~

    3.  ~~`predictNewSites(fitmodel)` for non-surveyed sites~~ --
        **done**: added to the vignette's "Prediction at new sites"
        section, deduplicating `data$info` by `Site` to build
        `X_psi_new`/`X_s_new` for the original sites as a worked example
        (see `44c0b03`); a simulation with known true occupancy
        probabilities confirmed `predictNewSites()` recovers truth about
        as well as the fitted estimate
        (`computePredictiveOccupancyProbs()`) does.

    4.  ~~fix `Xs` recycling bug in `simulateOccJSDMData()`
        (`R/simulateData.R`)~~ -- **fixed**: `data_info` now binds
        `Xs = Xs[idx_z_k,]` (indexed the same way as `X_psi`) in both
        the `"occupancy"` and `"two_stage"` branches, so
        `info$Xs.1`/`Xs.2` correctly repeat one coordinate pair per site
        across its replicate rows instead of recycling unrelated pairs.

    5.  ~~fix copy-paste bug in `computeMinESS()` (`R/diagnostics.R`,
        introduced in `af5ebe3`)~~ -- **fixed**: `computeMinESS()` now
        computes `ESS_beta0psi` from `beta0_psi_output` directly and
        includes it in the final `min()`

    6.  fix `runOccJSDM()` throwing `object 'M' not found` for
        `model %in% c("binary","continuous")` (JSDM-only) data: `M` (and
        `K`) are only ever assigned inside the
        `model %in% c("occupancy","two_stage")` branch (around line
        536-559 of `R/runOccJSDM.R`), but the final `infos` list (around
        line 1306/1308) references `M`/`K` unconditionally. Confirmed
        live: any JSDM-only fit (no `Site` column, or `Site` with no
        repeats) errors after MCMC completes, right when assembling
        `infos`. Fix: default `M <- rep(1, n)` / `K <- rep(1, n)` (or
        similar) in the `else` branch alongside where `siteNames <- 1:n`
        is already set for this case.

    7.  fix `simulateOccJSDMData(model = "occupancy")`
        (`R/simulateData.R`): its `data_info` construction binds
        `Site = idx_z_k`, `X_psi = X_psi[idx_z_k,]`,
        `Xs = Xs[idx_z_k,]`, but `idx_z_k` is only populated by
        `createDataIdx()` when `twostage = TRUE` -- for
        `model == "occupancy"` (`twostage = FALSE`), `idx_z_k` is
        `NULL`, so this errors with
        `arguments imply differing number of rows: 0, N`. Confirmed
        live. Fix: use `idx_z_w` (the per-sample site index, populated
        regardless of `twostage`) instead of `idx_z_k` for the
        `"occupancy"` branch. Workaround used this session: simulate via
        `model = "two_stage"` with trivial `P = 1`, `K = rep(1, N)`
        instead, which routes through the working `idx_z_k`-populated
        code path and produces an equivalent classical single-PCR-rep
        occupancy dataset.

2.  Doug to dos

    1.  ~~purge `data/traitdata_caiwang.rdata` from git history (need to
        coordinate with Alex because he will need to re-clone)~~ --
        **done**: purged via `git filter-repo` and force-pushed the
        rewritten `main` history to `origin` (`AlexDiana/occJSDM`). All
        commit hashes from `11c5449` onward changed as a result --
        **Alex needs to re-clone or hard-reset** his local copy, since
        his existing history is now divergent.

    2.  ~~model diagnostics functions from GLGS-eDNA repo~~ -- **done**:
        `computeRhat()`, `summarisePosterior()`, `plotTraceplot()`, and
        `returnConvergenceDiagnostics()` added to `R/diagnostics.R`.
        `computeRhat()`/`summarisePosterior()` were later de-exported
        (`@noRd`) since they're redundant with
        `returnConvergenceDiagnostics()` as user-facing functions; only
        `plotTraceplot()`/`returnConvergenceDiagnostics()` are exported.
        `compare_to_true()`/`plot_estimated_vs_true()` (simulation-truth
        comparison, requiring `true_params`) were **not** ported -- left
        as a possible future item. ~~A "Model diagnostics" vignette
        section... deferred~~ -- **done**: added to
        `vignettes/occJSDM.Rmd` (with a "Traceplots for a single
        covariate" subsection). A testthat test for `R/diagnostics.R` is
        still not done.

**MEE paper**

1.  Doug to dos

    1.  **reproduce all Ecoletts results as a test of the package and
        decide whether to include in repo**

2.  Alex to dos

    1.  Overleaf document for the occJSDM math, which we will add as a
        markdown vignette

    2.  GAMs for JSDM

    3.  ability to analyse count data

    4.  `plotCumulativeSpeciesDetections()` for different values of M.

    5.  scenario for source-sink inference (sites where env covariate
        coeffcients are negative but spatial covariate coefficients are
        positive), using an explicit source-sink simulation

    6.  remove effect of space on environmental covariates. remove the
        effect of unobserved environmental on observed environmental
        covariates and space. thus, adding factors (unobserved env
        covariates) doesn't change the effect of observed env covariates

    7.  **site** variance partitioning to complement the **species**
        variation partitioning (see Leibold et al., Cai et al.).
        Consider calling it **variation** partitioning.

**Outreach**

1.  Listserv announcement (beta release), drafted July 20 2026:

    > Subject: New R package (beta) — occJSDM, for eDNA occupancy/JSDM
    > modeling
    >
    > Hi all,
    >
    > Announcing **occJSDM**, an R package for jointly modeling eDNA
    > metabarcoding data across species
    > (<https://github.com/AlexDiana/occJSDM>). Note this is **beta
    > software**, under active development.
    >
    > occJSDM extends the two-stage eDNA occupancy model of Ji et al.
    > (2025, *Ecology Letters*, <doi:10.1111/ele.70302>) — which
    > separates field capture from PCR/lab detection error, per species
    > — by adding a joint species distribution model (JSDM) layer.
    > Highlights:
    >
    > - Models both false negatives and false positives at each
    >   detection stage (field capture and PCR/lab), per species.
    > - Integrates occupancy modeling with a full-featured JSDM: species
    >   fit jointly, with latent-factor residual correlations rather
    >   than one at a time.
    > - Supports species traits shaping occupancy responses, and
    >   optional spatial autocorrelation (GP kernel) across sites.
    > - MCMC fitting with diagnostics, variance partitioning,
    >   ordination, and residual correlation outputs built in.
    >
    > Vignettes included for simulation and model
    > fitting/interpretation. Feedback (and bug reports) very welcome.
    >
    > [Your name]

**Future versions**

1.  use spike-in to estimate abundance change (i.e. eDNAPlus)

2.  model selection of environmental, spatial covariates via
    regularisation/shrinkage, which would be useful with e.g. geospatial
    foundation model embeddings as env covariates

3.  parallelisation for speedup
